stories.headline: Russian airstrikes kill at least seven people in Ukraine overnight
stories.summary: Dnipro bore the brunt of the attacks but Odesa and Kharkiv were also targeted in largest onslaught for several daysRussian drone and missile strikes across Ukraine killed at least seven people overnight, including five in the city of Dnipro, Ukrainian local authorities have said.Reports say that at least 34 people have been injured in the strikes, which lasted “practically all night”, according to the Dnipropetrovsk regional head, Oleksandr Hanzh

stories.headline: Palestinians vote in West Bank and central Gaza municipal elections, the first since war started
stories.summary: The elections, held in the Israeli-occupied West Bank and in Gaza's Deir al-Balah, are taking place against the backdrop of a highly volatile political and security environment.

stories.headline: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
stories.summary: Local elections have been held in the occupied West Bank and in one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not taking part.

stories.headline: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
stories.summary: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

stories.headline: White House Sends Team to Pakistan as Iran Balks at Talks
stories.summary: President Donald Trump has sent envoys to Pakistan with the intention of meeting with Iranian officials, while Tehran sounded a pessimistic tone on the prospects for talks to end the eight-week war roiling the global economy. Bloomberg News Deputy UAE Bureau Chief Abeer Abu Omar, Washington Correspondent Josh Wingrove and Senior Reporter Dan Strumpf join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: West Bank and part of Gaza vote in first municipal elections since war
stories.summary: Municipal elections in the West Bank and central Gaza mark the first vote since the Gaza war, with Fatah-linked lists dominant and Hamas absent from the race.

stories.headline: Trump cancels trip by US negotiators for Iran peace talks
stories.summary: President said he was no longer sending Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan

stories.headline: Strait of Hormuz Remains Near-Empty With Just a Few Iran Ships Moving
stories.summary: The Strait of Hormuz remains largely empty of merchant ships, with only a few Tehran-linked vessels moving through the waterway, following a tense week that saw Iranian gunboat attacks and tanker interceptions by the US Navy.

stories.headline: Iran says no direct talks planned as U.S. envoys set to leave for Pakistan
stories.summary: Iran’s foreign minister arrived in Islamabad for talks with Pakistani officials. The White House said Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner would leave for Islamabad on Saturday.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy says he is ready to meet Putin in Azerbaijan
stories.summary: In a significant first visit to the South Caucasus since the start of Russia’s all-out war, Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said in a statement next to President Ilham Aliyev he is ready to meet Vladimir Putin in Azerbaijan after the US-led diplomatic talks have stalled over the past weeks.

stories.headline: Trump cancels US envoys’ trip after Iran’s Araghchi leaves Pakistan
stories.summary: Iranian state media reports foreign minister expected to return to Islamabad on Sunday night.

stories.headline: Trump cancels US negotiators' trip to Pakistan for Iran talks as Araghchi departs Islamabad
stories.summary: US special envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner were expected to arrive in Pakistan on Saturday to conduct a second round of peace talks between the US and Iran.

stories.headline: Russian strikes kill at least seven and injure dozens across Ukraine
stories.summary: Most of those killed and injured in Russian overnight attacks were in the eastern Ukrainian city of Dnipro, where an attack killed at least five and injured more than 30.

stories.headline: Mexico says US agents killed in crash weren't permitted to operate there
stories.summary: The two Americans who reportedly worked for the CIA died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab.

stories.headline: Thirty-one sloths die in Florida before opening of attraction
stories.summary: Many died due to conditions at a warehouse they'd been shipped to, while others arrived dead, authorities say.

stories.headline: Seven dead in major Russian attack on Ukraine
stories.summary: The city of Dnipro was hardest hit, with officials saying four died in a strike on a residential building.

stories.headline: Two killed in strike on alleged drug boat in Pacific Ocean, US military says
stories.summary: The incident is the latest in a series of US strikes on alleged drug trafficking boats.

stories.headline: EU Leaders Convene in Cyprus Amid Iran War
stories.summary: European countries need to step up strategically in the Middle East, strike deals with partners across the region and try to bring an end to the Iran war, Cyprus’ foreign minister said Friday. Bloomberg News Chief Europe Correspondent Oliver Crook joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Editor's Choice: Energy crisis drives India toward EVs as Asia's auto map shifts
Hungary’s Next Premier Warns Investors to Shun Orban-Tied Assets
stories.summary: Incoming Hungarian Prime Minister Peter Magyar said he had information that wealthy figures linked to Viktor Orban’s outgoing government were moving assets abroad and called on authorities to detain fleeing “oligarch” families.

stories.headline: What the Fed Can Do About Anthropic’s Latest System
stories.summary: Anthropic says its newest AI system can detect serious cybersecurity risks, but the new technology is so powerful that the company is holding back public release. The nearly autonomous system can detect banking software vulnerabilities and spawn sub-agents that operate without human oversight at unprecedented speed. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Fed Chair Jay Powell took the unusual step of calling in major bank leaders to discuss the risk

stories.headline: At White House Correspondents Dinner, Trump Will Dine With Reporters He’s Been Roasting
stories.summary: President Trump has boycotted the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in previous years.

stories.headline: Trump eager to show off latest White House project to King Charles
stories.summary: The president’s black granite path is the newest change to the West Wing, following gold lettering, Oval Office gilding and a “Presidential Walk of Fame.”

stories.headline: Explosion in southwest Colombia kills at leat seven, state governor says
stories.summary: Authorities in Cauca region demand 'decisive' government action after deadly explosion on Pan-American Highway.

stories.headline: Iran FM Abbas Araghchi and Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif meet in Pakistan
stories.summary: Iranian FM Araghchi met Pakistani PM Sharif in Islamabad as part of efforts to revive direct negotiations with the US.

stories.headline: Orbán steps down from Hungarian parliament after landslide defeat
stories.summary: The outgoing prime minister will not take up his seat after leading his party back into opposition.

stories.headline: The viral manifesto of 'anti-woke' tech boss with NHS and defence contracts
stories.summary: The boss of the controversial tech company with UK government contracts published a 22-point plan on the future of the West.

stories.headline: Katya Adler: Europe's Nato allies push back at reported US threat to Spain
stories.summary: Souring relations between Europe and the US have reared their Medusa-like head again, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

stories.headline: Banks brace for tax raid if Starmer is ousted
stories.summary: Executives prepare for shift to the left in UK government after narrow escape in last year’s Budget

stories.headline: No edge, no hedge: why markets are stuck
stories.summary: Neither extreme optimism nor extreme pessimism makes much sense, and neither is easy nor safe to express

stories.headline: Pro-independence parties bank on rethink of UK ‘status quo’ after May 7
stories.summary: Forecast gains for Scotland and Wales nationalists come even as no grouping has immediate roadmap for breaking away

stories.headline: Cheap Private Credit Funds Draw Bargain Hunters
stories.summary: Bargain hunters are snatching up private lending funds in the stock market, eager to buy assets that look cheap by a key measure.

stories.headline: Billion-Barrel Hormuz Oil Shock Is About to Crash Demand
stories.summary: The Strait of Hormuz oil shock has yet to crash demand as the rich world borrows from its stocks and pays up to secure supply. Traders are now sounding the alarm that a harsh adjustment is coming. Bloomberg News Washington Bureau Chief Peggy Collins and White House Correspondent Jeff Mason join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend for a wide-ranging conversation on the latest in politics. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Triumphal Arch: How Trump is Leaving His Mark on DC
stories.summary: Twice the size of the Lincoln Memorial, taller than almost all structures in Washington DC, and the winged figure atop rising some 60 feet high, the equivalent of a six-story building: President Donald Trump’s proposed “Triumphal Arch.” Bloomberg CityLab writer Kriston Capps joins Christina Ruffini in Washington DC to discuss the impact Trump is having on the architecture of the nation's capital. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: The Billion-Barrel Hormuz Oil Shock Is About to Crash Demand
stories.summary: The Strait of Hormuz oil shock has yet to crash demand as the rich world borrows from its stocks and pays up to secure supply. Traders are now sounding the alarm that a harsh adjustment is coming.

stories.headline: S&P's Yergin: "The Biggest Energy Disruption We've Ever Seen"
stories.summary: The Strait of Hormuz oil shock has yet to crash demand as the rich world borrows from its stocks and pays up to secure supply. Traders are now sounding the alarm that a harsh adjustment is coming. S&P Global Vice Chairman Daniel Yergin joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Decoding Muskism: Beyond the Billionaire
stories.summary: The world's richest man may soon also be the world's first trillionaire as Elon Musk's SpaceX targets an IPO with a valuation of around $1.5 Trillion in what would be the largest IPO ever. Muskism: A Guide for the Perplexed authors Ben Tarnoff and Quinn Slobodian join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss "Muskism," referring to the ideology and worldview that shaped Musk. They say it is "an operating system for th

stories.headline: Bitcoin’s Stealth Rally Has Traders Setting Sights on $80,000
stories.summary: Bitcoin is approaching $80,000 for the first time since January — a stealth recovery built not on euphoria but on short covering and the relentless accumulation by one company: Strategy Inc.

stories.headline: In California, a Voter ID Proposal Is Set to Appear on the Ballot in November
stories.summary: A Republican-backed initiative has cleared the signature threshold for the election. Critics say the proposal could make it harder for people to vote.

stories.headline: What counts as the woods? Judge axes Nova Scotia’s ban that defied ‘commonsense definitions’
stories.summary: The court sided with a Canadian hiker who deliberately challenged the order imposed to curb spread of wildfiresAs wildfires raged across Nova Scotia last summer, the Canadian province made a simple plea to residents: stay away from the woods.As the situation deteriorated, authorities turned the request into a prohibition: anyone caught hiking under the shade of the forest canopy faced a C$25,000 fine – a figure more than half the average worker’s

stories.headline: It costs $230 out-of-pocket to see a dermatologist. Many Australians go without – despite soaring skin cancer rates
stories.summary: Exclusive: Report shows cost of first appointment rose $20 in one year, with steeper rises in Western Australia, South Australia and TasmaniaGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastPatients are increasingly going without medically necessary dermatological care, the head of the Consumers Health Forum said, as new report reveals the rising cost of the specialty in Australia.Dermatology is expensive and getting pricier, with an av

stories.headline: Cory Bernardi repays more than $40,000 for flying on Gina Rinehart’s plane during SA election campaign
stories.summary: Former Liberal party senator, who was elected to state upper house in March, says flights ‘worth every cent’Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastOne Nation’s South Australian leader has paid back Australia’s richest person for private flights he took while campaigning in the state’s recent election.Cory Bernardi confirmed on Saturday that he had reimbursed a “substantial” sum of money to Gina Rinehart’s company S Kidman &amp

stories.headline: Protestor depicts Israeli minister Ben Gvir covered in blood in Stockholm
stories.summary: Activists organised a protest in solidarity with Palestine on Saturday in Stockholm, Sweden.

stories.headline: France defends abstention on UN slave trade resolution
stories.summary: France justified abstaining on a UN resolution recognising the slave trade as the gravest crime against humanity.

stories.headline: Joy and desire for change as Gaza’s Deir el-Balah holds elections
stories.summary: Deir el-Balah residents vote for first time since 2006 despite destruction, displacement and genocide.

stories.headline: Islamabad residents bear cost of Pakistan's US-Iran mediation
Bank branches in Japan tapped to double as luggage hubs
Uzbekistan pushes digital, aiming to formalize shadow economy
Nvidia not competing with clients on AI models: vice president
Prudential 'still very committed' to Japan market: CEO
Florida officials investigate planned ‘Sloth World’ attraction after 31 sloths die in warehouse
stories.summary: Languorous tree dwellers from Guyana and Peru died from ‘cold stun’ in warehouse with no power or running waterSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailWildlife officials in Florida said in a newly released report that dozens of sloths taken from South American rainforests for display at a controversial new tourist attraction in Orlando died in the care of their new owners.An incident report from the Florida fish and wildlife conservation

stories.headline: EU leaders vow to boost security and economic ties with Middle East to minimise effect of Iran war
stories.summary: Leaders from Lebanon, Egypt, Syria and Jordan as well as the secretary general of the Gulf Cooperation Council, were in Nicosia to meet their European Union counterparts on the sidelines of an EU summit.

stories.headline: Philippines' Duterte to stand trial as ICC confirms crimes against humanity charges
stories.summary: Ex-president Rodrigo Duterte is accused of the extrajudicial killing of thousands during his "war on drugs".

stories.headline: Thousands at risk after multi-million dollar Everest flood warning system left to rust
stories.summary: The flood warning system at Imja glacial lake has not been maintained since 2016, fearful locals tell BBC.

stories.headline: US justice department drops probe into Fed chairman Jerome Powell
stories.summary: President Donald Trump had accused Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed's building.

stories.headline: Why is this game only legal across Australia one day a year?
stories.summary: Two-up is a coin toss betting game that was played by Australian soldiers during World War One - it’s now only legal across Australia on 25 April.

stories.headline: Woman trapped in poo for three hours after outback toilet collapses
stories.summary: The woman got stuck waist-deep in a pit latrine in the Australian outback after it gave way.

stories.headline: US to allow firing squads, gas and electrocution for federal executions
stories.summary: In a memo, the Department of Justice says the move will "strengthen" the death penalty and deter crime.

stories.headline: Pirates hijack oil tanker off the coast of Somalia
stories.summary: Piracy, once notorious in this stretch of the Indian Ocean, has made a comeback in recent years.

stories.headline: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer
stories.summary: The Israeli prime minister says he is "in excellent physical condition" after having a malignant tumour removed.

stories.headline: Headscarf with a beret: Muslim designers showcase floral dresses and boxy streetwear in Paris
stories.summary: Some young attendees say the event demonstrated a more inclusive French culture taking shape.

stories.headline: The Kashmir town trying to win back tourists after a deadly attack
stories.summary: A year after the Pahalgam attack, tourism has slowed in Indian-administered Kashmir, hitting lives and livelihoods.

stories.headline: Why police are seeking to arrest billionaire K-pop mogul behind BTS
stories.summary: Bang Si-hyuk, who created the supergroup, denies defrauding investors before his $7.3bn company went public.

stories.headline: ‘Look, no hands’: China chases the driverless dream at Beijing car show
stories.summary: As domestic sales slow, manufacturers are investing in AI and seeking growth in technology and in overseas marketsAt the world’s biggest car fair, which opened in Beijing on Friday, there were hundreds of manufacturers, more than 1,000 vehicles, hundreds of thousands of enthusiasts – and hardly anyone behind a wheel.China’s car companies have cornered the domestic electric vehicle market, and are increasingly visible on the global stage. Now they

stories.headline: Anthony Albanese accused of ‘caving to gas companies’ as Labor set to reject new export tax
stories.summary: David Pocock says prime minister – who is trying to shore up fuel supplies – is parroting industry talking pointsFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastLabor is poised to reject a growing push for a new 25% tax on gas exports in next month’s budget, prompting David Pocock to accuse the government of “caving in” to the gas industry.It’s understood the government has elected

stories.headline: UK House of Lords accused of 'obstructionism' as time runs out on assisted dying bill
stories.summary: MPs in the House of Commons had backed legalising euthanasia for adults who have been given less than six months to live and can clearly express a wish to die, in a historic vote last June.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill eight Palestinians in Gaza, first responders say
stories.summary: One strike killed three children and two adults near a mosque in the northern town of Beit Lahia on Wednesday evening.

stories.headline: Philippines’ ex-president Rodrigo Duterte to face trial for crimes against humanity
stories.summary: ICC judges say there are substantial grounds to believe Duterte guided anti-drugs crackdown that killed thousandsThe former president of the Philippines, Rodrigo Duterte, will face trial at the international criminal court (ICC) after judges unanimously confirmed charges of crimes against humanity over his “war on drugs”.Pre-trial judges concluded on Thursday that there were substantial grounds to believe Duterte was responsible for the crimes ag

stories.headline: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
stories.summary: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.

stories.headline: Iran says Strait of Hormuz cannot be opened due to ceasefire breaches
stories.summary: Iran's chief negotiator says "violations" by the US and Israel make it impossible to open the strait.

stories.headline: Woman killed by bear in Polish forest, son and local government say
stories.summary: In an extremely rare fatal encounter, the 58-year-old was killed as she was walking in a mountainous area, her son told rescuers.

stories.headline: Taiwan minister makes rare visit to disputed South China Sea island
stories.summary: Kuan Bi-ling observed coast guard drills on Taiping Island, also claimed by China, the Philippines and Vietnam.

stories.headline: High-stakes West Bengal election begins as parties row over voter roll
stories.summary: The polls are taking place against a controversy over a sweeping revision of electoral rolls.

stories.headline: US and Iran in blockade standoff as Pakistan pushes for talks
stories.summary: The mood in the Strait of Hormuz remains combustible despite Trump's ceasefire extension.

stories.headline: Tracking ships under fire in the Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Iran's top negotiator says it's "not possible to reopen" the strait due to "blatent violations" of US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.headline: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds
stories.summary: Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather.At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researche

stories.headline: Moldovan oligarch jailed in $1bn 'theft of the century' case
stories.summary: Vlad Plahotniuc, once Moldova's richest man, was found guilty of partaking in a fraud which involved around 12% of the country's GDP.

stories.headline: South Korean fighter jets collided due to pilots taking pictures, report finds
stories.summary: One of the pilots has been fined thousands of dollars by the military to cover repair costs.

stories.headline: Hotpot, bubble tea and sportswear: China's new exports take on the world
stories.summary: Made in China used to mean cheap, mass-produced goods but a new wave of Chinese brands is emerging.

stories.headline: India has splurged billions on metro trains. But where are the commuters?
stories.summary: Without better last-mile connectivity and affordable fares, metro use is unlikely to improve quickly, say experts.

stories.headline: Chernobyl's last wedding: The couple who married as a nuclear disaster unfolded
stories.summary: Serhiy and Iryna married 40 years ago, unaware a nuclear reactor had exploded less than 3 miles away.
